_comment: Per-OEM driver / SoftPaq catalog access patterns. Used by the drivers module to resolve a device's subsystem-vendor OEM to a downloadable installer. Fields: oem (matches oem_pci_vendors.json vendor name), catalogType (softpaq|per-machine|per-chip|driver-portal), urlPattern (templated — {id}, {mt}, {tag} etc. are runtime-filled), machineIdRequired (bool — some OEMs gate downloads behind machine serial / machine-type), notes.
_crossVendorNote: HP SoftPaqs are historically the most cross-vendor-friendly for Realtek WLAN/BT chipsets — a Lenovo/HP-vendored Realtek card (subsystem 103C) can only get current drivers via HP's catalog even on a Lenovo chassis. See knowledge_base/session_2026-07-04_lenovo_slim7prox.md problem 1.

sources.urlPatternNotes: SoftPaqs are numbered globally, hosted in bucket ranges of 500. For sp162860: lower=162501, upper=163000 → https://ftp.hp.com/pub/softpaq/sp162501-163000/sp162860.exe
sources.notes: Most cross-vendor-friendly SoftPaqs for Realtek WLAN/BT + Synaptics. Numbering rolls forward monotonically. Verify actual sp id via product page, not by guessing the number.
sources.verify: URL pattern for bucket boundaries — HP occasionally shifts bucket sizes

sources.urlPatternNotes: Machine Type (MT, first 4 chars of the serial's model code, e.g. 82V2 for Slim 7 ProX 14ARH7) is required. Newer System Update-driven catalog at https://download.lenovo.com/pccbbs/mobiles/{catalog_id}.txt
sources.notes: Won't ship a driver for a subsystem vendor that isn't Lenovo (e.g. HP-vendored Realtek card in a Lenovo chassis gets nothing). Check subsystem-vendor OEM first, then fall back to Lenovo only if it matches.
sources.verify: Machine Type resolution — sometimes MT is 4-char, sometimes 5

sources.urlPatternNotes: Service Tag (7-char, from BIOS) is the key. Also accepts Express Service Code. Direct downloads under downloads.dell.com/FOLDER{id}M/1/{driver}.EXE — folder path not stable long-term.
sources.notes: Signs many of its own drivers (repackaged Realtek/Intel etc.). Service Tag lookup returns a manifest JSON that lists direct .exe URLs.
Model-family driven. Bundles most driver installers behind an interactive picker; no clean static URL pattern.

Realtek's public download site publishes only a limited/older WLAN + audio driver set. For a current Realtek WLAN/BT driver, prefer an HP SoftPaq. Realtek Audio (Realtek Audio Console / UAD) is the exception — Realtek publishes current builds directly.

sources.urlPatternNotes: Intel's Download Center uses a productId per driver family. Intel Driver & Support Assistant (DSA) resolves it automatically.
sources.notes: Intel WLAN (AX2xx), Bluetooth, Chipset, iGPU — all resolve cleanly. DSA is the quickest programmatic path. Windows Update also stays current on Intel WLAN, unlike Realtek.

sources.urlPatternNotes: GPU model + OS + branch (Game Ready / Studio) form the query. GeForce Experience app automates it. NVIDIA also publishes an official JSON manifest at https://gfwsl.geforce.com/services_toolkit/services/com/nvidia/services/AjaxDriverService.php.
sources.notes: Laptop drivers (mobile GPUs) are OEM-scoped historically, but NVIDIA has unified this — modern .exe installers work across OEMs on GeForce mobile.

sources.urlPatternNotes: AMD Adrenalin auto-detect app resolves the exact package. Manual selector by product family + model.
sources.notes: AMD publishes both consumer Adrenalin and OEM-branded (pro / laptop) drivers. Laptop iGPU drivers may lag OEM builds — always check the chassis OEM's catalog first for mobile Radeon.
